description: An integer overflow vulnerability in V8's TypedArray bounds checking allows bypassing the V8 sandbox. In CopyElementsHandleImpl (src/objects/elements.cc), the bounds check `CHECK_LE(offset + length, destination_ta->GetLengthOrOutOfBounds(...))` does not guard against integer overflow of the `size_t` addition `offset + length`. When called via Runtime_TypedArraySet (src/runtime/runtime-typedarray.cc) with attacker-controlled `length` and `offset` values that are individually valid but together overflow a size_t, the wrapped-around sum bypasses the CHECK_LE guard. This allows the subsequent data pointer arithmetic (`destination->DataPtr() + offset * element_size`) to reference memory outside the V8 sandbox address space, resulting in an out-of-sandbox write. The vulnerability is triggered through TypedArray.prototype.set() (or directly via %TypedArraySet with patched argument count) by supplying a large offset and length whose sum overflows.

fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6285402 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
